author: govinda dńĀsa
verses: campaka ŇõoŠĻáa kusuma kanakńĀcala jitala gaura‚čÖtanu‚čÖlńĀbaŠĻái re unnata gńęma sńęma nńĀhi anubhava jaga‚čÖmana‚čÖmohana bhńĀŠĻÖanńę re jaya Ňõacńę‚čÖnandana tribhuvana‚čÖvandana kali‚čÖyuga‚čÖkńĀla‚čÖbhujaga‚čÖbhaya‚čÖkhaŠĻáŠłćana re vipula pulaka kula ńĀkula kalevara gara gara antare prema‚čÖbhare lahu lahu hńĀsanńę gada gada bhńĀŠĻ£aŠĻáńę kata mandńĀkinńę nayane jhare nija‚čÖrase nńĀcatanayana ŠłćhulńĀyata gńĀyata kata kata bhakatahiŠĻĀ meli jo rase bhńĀsi avaŇõa mahńę‚čÖmaŠĻáŠłćala govinda‚čÖdńĀsa tahiŠĻĀ paraŇõa nńĀ bheli
en_translation:
The beauty of Gaura's golden body conquers the beauty of the reddish flowers and the golden mountain.

His raised neck is charming beyond limit, and His demeanor enchants the minds of all in the world.

All glories to the Son of Ňöacńę! He is worshipped by the three worlds and is the destroyer of the fear of the serpent of the Age of Kali.

His entire body is covered with goosebumps from ecstasy, and His heart is overwhelmed with divine love.

His gentle smile, His faltering speech, and the countless streams of tears that flow from His eyes!

He dances in His own transcendental mellows, His eyes rolling, and He sings together with many devotees.

In that mellow, the entire earth floats, completely overwhelmed. Only Govinda DńĀsa could not attain that experience.
